core_policy: Treat every Chromium version change as a controlled rebase with a new candidate runtime version; never silently roll the base under an existing release claim.
Build an unpatched baseline first, then apply BrowseForge patches, so upstream detector drift is separable from BrowseForge regressions.
A source patch that still applies proves only source compatibility; anti-detection compatibility requires a rebuilt artifact and live detector evidence.
Package/SBOM/provenance success is not launch success; launch success is not detector success; single-platform detector success is not cross-platform success.
Keep high-risk spoof surfaces switch-gated, bounded, fail-closed, and suppress partial spoofing in strict native mode where native coherence is safer.
Record every accepted caveat explicitly in manifests and release notes; do not promote alpha caveats to production claims.

framework_findings.evidence: scripts/apply_stealth_scaffold.py --check now verifies copied scaffold files and root BUILD.gn //browseforge/stealth dependency.
framework_findings.finding: Patch/scaffold check mode must distinguish applicability from applied state.

framework_findings.evidence: scripts/chromium_source.py preflight_step now compares git_head(step.cwd) to plan.base_commit before verify-pinned-commit subprocess execution.
framework_findings.finding: Pinned commit verification must fail when HEAD differs from manifest base_commit.

framework_findings.evidence: scripts/chromium_source.py run_step no longer skips generate-dev-build when build.ninja already exists.
framework_findings.finding: GN output must not be trusted solely because build.ninja exists.

framework_findings.evidence: patch-best-practice-audit records per-patch watch files/symbols and requires semantic review on anchor drift.
framework_findings.finding: Text anchors remain noisy across Chromium upgrades.

hard_blockers: patch anchor repaired without reviewing the new upstream symbol semantics
high-risk surface changed without rebuilt artifact detector evidence
WebRTC DOM candidate masking claimed as network leak prevention without network/proxy evidence
feature inventory enabled without API/permission/service coherence evidence
fonts or WebGL string spoofing claimed as complete surface coverage without metric/parameter/render evidence
release-grade claim while any supported production platform lacks launch/detector/signing evidence
audio noise active mode changes WebAudio getChannelData backing-array mutation semantics until redesigned or proven safe
browser-side UA-CH request metadata is not yet proven coherent with Blink DOM uaData overrides
WebRTC candidate SDP related-address rewriting is not yet coherent enough for standalone leak-prevention claims
feature parity flags are alpha DOM-inventory only until service/permission behavior is validated

checks: inspect src/build/vs_toolchain.py TOOLCHAIN_HASH and SDK_VERSION for Windows SDK changes
phase_order.objective: Choose a Chromium target tag and record immutable base metadata before mutating source.
Build and test the new upstream Chromium without BrowseForge patches.
required_outputs: sanitized baseline detector evidence when detector drift analysis is in scope

checks: unit tests for changed patch scripts
launcher validation tests for every emitted switch
phase_order.objective: Reapply BrowseForge patch scripts against the new upstream and repair only with source-aware semantic review.
required_outputs: updated patch scripts/tests if anchors move
GN check evidence for touched targets

checks: macOS x64 package or explicit caveat
Windows x64 cross-compile via windows-toolchain-sop and manual OS validation
phase_order.objective: Produce platform artifacts from the rebased source and package with provenance.
Prove anti-detection surfaces with live detector evidence on rebuilt artifacts.

platform_order: windows-x64 cross-compile plus manual Windows OS validation
purpose: Durable strategy for upgrading the pinned Chromium base while preserving BrowseForge anti-detection claims through evidence, not assumptions.
repo_update_contract: Update knowledge/manifests/patchset.json for every source patch status change.
Regenerate release-status/objective-audit after manifest input changes.
Refresh BrowseForge Chromium Runtime Knowledge Base and codebase-memory KG, then commit .codebase-memory artifacts.
